methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $200M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $600M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.1x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Profluent
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series B), post-money $600M

data.narrative:
Science Corporation, a Series B biotech company founded by Max Hodak, is evaluated against a comp universe of 3 public peers (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private-stage companies in Biotech & Health, including Eikon Therapeutics, Neko Health, Asimov, and Profluent.

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis uses the company's $200M+ total raised as a proxy base value and applies a 1.9x multiplier typical for Series B advancement, implying a $380M valuation. Peer-median analysis draws from 4 same-stage, same-sector comparables with a median valuation of $1.3B, ranging from $600M (Profluent) to $3.3B (Eikon Therapeutics). Public-comp anchoring using the sector's 6.1x P/S multiple cannot be applied due to the absence of target revenue disclosure, standard for private companies.

Triangulating the two applicable methods yields a valuation range of $323M (low) to $1.5B (high), with a median of $840M. The wide spread reflects material dispersion in peer valuations and the limited granularity of stage-progression methodology relative to peer comparables. Key uncertainties include Science Corporation's capital efficiency relative to the peer set, technology differentiation relative to Eikon and Neko Health at the high end, and clinical or commercial inflection points that may justify positioning within the range.
